idea.hook: Think humanity's energy use is immense?
facts: All 8 billion humans, all industries, consume ~5.95 x 10^20 Joules of primary energy *per year* (2021 data).
The Sun produces an astounding 3.8 x 10^26 Joules of energy *every single second*.
idea.payoff: The Sun generates ALL of Earth's yearly primary energy needs in less than 2 microseconds (0.000002 seconds)!
idea.title: The Sun's Power Is UNBELIEVABLE! 🤯

scenes.text: Humanity's energy use is HUGE, right?
scenes.visual: Dark space background. Earth slowly rotates into view from the right, its city lights glowing brightly.

scenes.text: Earth's yearly energy demand: 5.95 x 10^20 Joules
scenes.visual: Close-up on Earth, showing a complex network of illuminated grid lines or energy consumption points animating across continents. The numbers fade in with the visual.

scenes.text: The Sun's output: 3.8 x 10^26 Joules PER SECOND!
scenes.visual: Seamless transition from Earth to a majestic, fiery Sun. Intense solar flares erupt, and powerful energy waves radiate outwards. The numbers slide in from the left.

scenes.text: How long for the Sun to power Earth's ENTIRE yearly needs?
scenes.visual: Split screen visual. Left side shows a stylized Earth with 'YEARLY ENERGY' text. Right side shows a glowing Sun with 'ENERGY PER SECOND' text. A large question mark graphic animates between them.

The Sun fills the entire screen, pulsating intensely with visible solar flares. Earth is a tiny, distant speck in the upper corner, completely dwarfed. A rapid numerical countdown (e.g., '0.000005s... 0.000004s...') flashes briefly before the main text dramatically pulses onto the screen.
